column1: pKa (justifies f_d=~1), membrane permeability P_d, aqueous diffusivity D_aq, and anion-partitioning notes. NOTE: log Kow is ill-defined for permanent anions - use log D or the C4 membrane/protein K values.

column4: value
~ -0.5 to ~1 (consensus); ESI-MS <=1; Kutsuna fit 1.3
column11: JUSTIFIES f_d=~1. Goss: fate-modeling pKa of 2.8 is ~3 log units too high; true value near -0.5. Shorter PFCAs (TFA) slightly higher (~0.3) but still strong acids.

column4: 3.8 +/- 0.1 (monomeric); ~2.3 at high conc (aggregation)
column11: Methodologically DISPUTED (mixed-solvent extrapolation, aggregation). Included for transparency: even pKa 3.8 gives f_d=0.94-0.999 at paddy pH 5-7, so f_d=~1 holds regardless.

column4: < 0 (strong sulfonic acids)
column11: PFSAs fully dissociated under all environmental conditions -> f_d=~1 trivially.

column4: >= 0.9999 for pKa<=1, pH 5-7; >=0.94 even for pKa=3.8
column11: Supports your assumption (A2/A3/A9): PFAS treated as PERMANENTLY dissociated anions. Robust across the pKa controversy AND across paddy porewater pH.

column4: low for the anion (per-congener values in source Table 2 / SI)
column11: DIRECT input to your GHK / diffusive root-uptake term. Low passive permeability of the anion is consistent with anion exclusion + the need for carrier/channel transport (see C5). Also reports K_MW (see C4).

column4: log K_SW(anion) linearly related to log K_SW(neutral) via Born-Haber cycle
column11: Use to estimate anion membrane/octanol partition where only neutral data exist. Confirms anion-dominant partitioning (consistent with f_d=~1).

column4: 2.5e-5 (PFBA) to 0.45e-5 (PFHxS)
column8: Measurement of Aqueous Diffusivities for PFAAs, 2019
column11: Input for diffusion/dispersion in xylem and soil porewater. Wilke-Chang does NOT capture the trend (non-uniform vs molar volume) - use measured values.

column4: pore/surface-D ratio 13 (PFHxS) to 0.88 (PFOS)
column8: Diffusion of PFAAs through clay-rich soil, 2021
column11: For your HYDRUS soil diffusion term: pure pore-diffusion UNDER-predicts long-chain (PFOS) transport - add a surface-diffusion-like process.

column4: ill-defined for permanent anions; literature values method-dependent
column11: Do NOT plug literature 'log Kow' into neutral-compound QSARs. Use log D (pH-corrected) or the measured membrane/protein K from the C4 tab.

column4: combines K_MW + K_prot to predict organ distribution
column11: Mammalian, but the equilibrium-partition framework transfers directly to your B_k-based tissue distribution (a useful template).

column4: what it gives
PFOA pKa ~ -0.5; the key reference justifying f_d=~1.
Membrane permeability P_d (+ K_MW) for PFAAs & alternatives - direct GHK input.
Spectroscopic PFOA pKa ~ 0 (independent confirmation).
Anion-vs-neutral partitioning model (Born-Haber); log D approach for PFAS.
Measured D_aq: 2.5e-5 (PFBA) to 0.45e-5 cm^2/s (PFHxS).
Equilibrium tissue-distribution framework (K_MW + K_prot) - model template.
Contested PFOA pKa 3.8 (for transparency on the controversy).
recommended action
Adopt pKa ~0-1 (PFCA), <0 (PFSA) -> f_d=~1; cite Goss 2008. No further work needed.

column4: Use Ebert 2020 P_d as surrogate; sensitivity-test the GHK/diffusive term across its range

column2: Partial - measured set is perfluoro C3-C8
column3: Measured C3-C8 trend (do NOT use Wilke-Chang)

Ignore volatilization for submerged paddy (anion, non-volatile).
